note: Aliases are pure sugar: they resolve to canonical IRIs BEFORE a claim is built, so the signed wire form always carries the full IRI. This file is content-addressed and federates like any record; a consumer may remap it. An alias binding is itself expressible as a signed owl:sameAs claim. Vocabulary policy (DECISIONS §20, §21): reuse a PUBLISHED ontology wherever one fits (PROV, PAV, DCAT, OWL, SKOS, and the W3C Security Vocabulary sec: for key->principal control - a signed key binding is a lightweight Verifiable Credential); mint kton-native IRIs (nk:) only for concepts no standard covers; and RESERVE the regulated gxp: terms for actual GxP-validated processes - ordinary review uses the general pav: terms, never gxp:.
